jokes.text: Love, peace, and hair grease!
jokes.punchline: Unexpected 'hair grease' addition to the cliché
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay on familiar phrase, economical but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: that bar where the waiters are dressed like ninjas
jokes.punchline: Absurd specificity of ninja-themed service
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist world-building, specific and visual

jokes.text: Donuts and then bed. What are you depressed about or celebrating?
jokes.punchline: Recognition that late-night donut consumption only happens for extreme emotional states
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice and relatable observation, highly quotable

jokes.text: How could you cut 'Diaper Chicken'?
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous sketch name
jokes.explanation: Typical 30 Rock fake sketch name absurdity

jokes.text: My ankle! Oh, 'Imaginary injury.' I would have said 'death of a voice coach.'
jokes.punchline: Writers immediately categorize and escalate the fake drama
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on sitcom tropes with perfect escalation

jokes.text: don't you have some gallery opening or a fundraiser to give bow ties to inner city youths?
jokes.setup: Asking Jack about his Friday night plans
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific charity involving bow ties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy characterization with absurd specificity

jokes.text: I just need somewhere I can socialize where women aren't an issue.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his need to avoid temptation
jokes.punchline: Ironic setup for going to gay bar
jokes.explanation: Sets up the situation with character logic

jokes.text: I know how to check your voice mail. You are officially a 'D-bag', Tim. I hope you lose your indoor soccer game.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to wishing failure at indoor soccer
jokes.explanation: Perfect millennial breakup anger with specific detail

jokes.text: Should you be working the night shift alone like this? It's fine. They gave me a gun.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being armed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, excellent economy

jokes.text: You want a dozen donuts to stay?
jokes.punchline: Worker's confusion at the unusual request
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist moment highlighting Liz's odd behavior

jokes.text: Making a new friend. I don't like that you have crazy-eyes. Oh, shut up, mouth.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to befriend pregnant girl
jokes.punchline: Talks to herself about her own crazy appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon self-awareness and desperation

jokes.text: What do your dads tell their friends you do? Surgeon. Optometrist. Died. I never really knew my dad, so... I just lie to myself. Space assassin.
jokes.setup: Question about what their fathers say about their comedy writing careers
jokes.punchline: Escalates through lies to 'space assassin'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with absurd 'space assassin'

jokes.text: Last time I saw my dad, he went out to get the candles for my fourth birthday lasagna... and never came back.
jokes.punchline: Birthday 'lasagna' instead of cake
jokes.explanation: Touching moment made funny by unexpected food choice

jokes.text: In Sicilian dialect, it means 'well poisoner.' In Gaelic, Donaghy means 'dung basket.'
jokes.punchline: Both names have terrible meanings
jokes.explanation: Great character bonding moment with specific humor

jokes.text: His contract is up, and I don't want to feel sorry for him.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of avoiding empathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy callousness delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Meanwhile, his MyFace page - horny.
jokes.punchline: Checking his social media status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing millennial relationship issues

jokes.text: When I was your age, you could just be like, 'Oh, he probably tried to call me, but my line was busy.' And then just... watch Falcon Crest and cry yourself to sleep.
jokes.punchline: Specific show reference and pathetic ending
jokes.explanation: Great generational humor with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: They all just seemed really old and weird. Not one of them even knew who Ne-Yo is.
jokes.punchline: Using Ne-Yo knowledge as compatibility metric
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about generational gaps

jokes.text: Finally! Thank you! Somebody cool, for once.
jokes.punchline: Thinks Liz is cool for knowing the song
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Liz's obvious uncoolness

jokes.text: Goodbye, my friend. He had to be mean to save him. Harry belonged in the woods.
jokes.punchline: Emotional reaction to silly movie, sets up episode theme
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central metaphor

jokes.text: Maybe that's why we're drawn to movies where the father figure pushes away the child/legendary North American forest ape.
jokes.punchline: Includes Bigfoot in psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd addition to psychological observation

jokes.text: I don't have a birth certificate because I was born inside of Yankee Stadium.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation for no birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdity with specific detail

jokes.text: And because of my drinking, they're often a surprise.
jokes.punchline: Admits to blackout drinking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor contrasting with Kenneth's innocent persona

jokes.text: This morning it hit me in the shower why the Hendersons named their guest 'Harry.'
jokes.setup: Jack having deep thoughts about the movie
jokes.punchline: Treating silly movie with academic seriousness
jokes.explanation: Jack's intellectual pretension applied to ridiculous subject

jokes.text: We both have recurring dreams about being overpowered by a female bodybuilder.
jokes.setup: Jack listing things he has in common with Frank
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific shared dream
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and inappropriate overshare

jokes.text: Make your father hate any new children he might have.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Frank to make his father proud
jokes.punchline: Dark twist about making father regret other kids
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Jack delivery

jokes.text: fake vomit on it. Right. Fake.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions fake vomit on Frank's desk
jokes.punchline: Frank's awkward confirmation suggests it's real
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good timing

jokes.text: Oh, God. Please don't be a daughter I didn't know about.
jokes.setup: Young woman arrives looking for him
jokes.punchline: Jack's first thought is secret daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack paranoia and lifestyle implications

jokes.text: So, do you want me to start Youth Consulting now? Because kids would not be into that.
jokes.setup: Being given a youth consulting role
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates youth perspective by rejecting the idea
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about corporate youth consulting

jokes.text: My heart goes out to all the inner city kids. Especially those too fat to dance their way out.
jokes.punchline: Horribly specific and inappropriate addition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna obliviousness and inappropriate specificity

jokes.text: Look at Biz Markie or The Doors.
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable pairing of Biz Markie and The Doors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison, very quotable

jokes.text: And when my caveman brain saw those babies... with their little Hornberger foreheads... it clicked.
jokes.punchline: Genetic specificity of 'Hornberger foreheads'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with visual specificity

jokes.text: My pregnant teen message board says the baby gets all its food from the mother. So, you have to give it stuff it likes.
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding how nutrition works
jokes.explanation: Good character ignorance moment

jokes.text: I'm going to post a reply to Juno 32 and tell her she's a giant ass wipe.
jokes.punchline: Online retaliation with movie reference username
jokes.explanation: Good millennial behavior with cultural reference

jokes.text: You already dress like a mom.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect insult disguised as compliment

jokes.text: What a schmo, entering first. Now, I'm the headliner, and he's just the warm-up act. I'm Mr. Don Rickles and he's just me.
jokes.punchline: Confusing analogy where she demotes herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism with confused logic

jokes.text: Did you know that if you sing 'Happy Birthday' on a TV show, you have to pay for it?
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about copyright issues
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about TV production reality

jokes.text: No one ask me about my back brace.
jokes.punchline: Immediately drawing attention to what she doesn't want discussed
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Jenna's attention-seeking behavior

jokes.text: I also would have accepted, 'You can't prove that's the governor's semen.'
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific alternative answer
jokes.explanation: Hilariously dark and specific legal corruption joke

jokes.text: And who knows when February 24th will come again?
jokes.setup: Tracy being sad birthday is over
jokes.punchline: Not understanding annual calendar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan logic gap

jokes.text: Someday, you'll have an ad on the subway in English and Spanish.
jokes.setup: Mother being proud of Frank becoming a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Her specific vision of success being ambulance chaser ads
jokes.explanation: Great character work - working class mother's specific idea of legal success

jokes.text: For the mob! Frankie's father didn't abandon him. He's in hiding in Phoenix.
jokes.punchline: Father isn't absent, he's in witness protection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist completely recontextualizing Frank's story

jokes.text: You want me to paint you a picture? Because I did. It's part of a therapy through painting thing.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation leads to therapy confession
jokes.explanation: Good literalization of metaphor with character detail

jokes.text: Don't even ask about the wheelchair.
jokes.punchline: Escalated from back brace to wheelchair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of running gag

jokes.text: There was once a great American named George Henderson. He met a woodland ape, or Sasquatch, who, despite its dangerous message of environmentalism, became his friend.
jokes.setup: Jack giving life advice through movie metaphor
jokes.punchline: Treating Bigfoot movie as profound and noting environmental message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analysis treating silly movie with academic seriousness

jokes.text: Is that Harry and the Hendersons? You've seen it? This is my life, Jack.
jokes.setup: Jack's philosophical speech about the movie
jokes.punchline: Liz realizes the absurdity of her situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment where Liz realizes she's living the movie

jokes.text: I wished for you to get better. I was going to wish for breakfast in bed with RoboCop while that elephant paints us.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific fantasy involving RoboCop and painting elephant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdist wish with surprising sweetness

jokes.text: But who would I celebrate with if y'all were in a car accident?
jokes.setup: Sweet moment about making Jenna's birthday wish come true
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately imagines everyone dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism turning sweet moment dark

jokes.text: Ugh! Fine, Lithgow! I'll do the right thing. God! I guess someone's been watching The World According to Garp.
jokes.setup: Liz deciding to do the right thing
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall with John Lithgow reference
jokes.explanation: Meta reference to show addressing John Lithgow directly

jokes.text: Do you know how many people want what just got dropped in your lap? 'Oh, now is not a good time. I want to go to Burning Man.' Shut up, Tim!
jokes.punchline: Specific millennial excuse about Burning Man
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: And get married and have disposable cameras at the wedding because it's fun and people like it.
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and outdated wedding detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - her idea of wedding advice includes oddly specific and slightly dated details

jokes.text: Get out of here! Can't you see we don't want you anymore? Go to the writers' room! There is no scholarship!
jokes.setup: Jack has to crush Frank's dreams
jokes.punchline: Echoing Harry and the Hendersons dialogue
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff using movie dialogue structure

jokes.text: Get out of here! Can't you see we don't want you anymore?
jokes.punchline: Using exact words from Harry and the Hendersons
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - transforms movie dialogue into real emotional moment, brilliant writing structure

jokes.text: Goodbye, my friend.
jokes.setup: Frank walking away after Jack's rejection
jokes.punchline: Direct quote from Harry and the Hendersons
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that gives episode its title

jokes.text: My God. Look at your boobs.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate first comment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tim's immaturity

jokes.text: I guess, in a way... we both... lost children today. Yeah, but mine was real, Jack. Yours was Frank.
jokes.punchline: Liz pointing out the absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz grounding Jack's pretension with reality

jokes.text: Can someone please tell me how to get out of this building? It's like a maze! I keep walking past the same... Sbarro's!
jokes.setup: Frank trying to leave the building
jokes.punchline: Lost in building with multiple Sbarro's locations
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist ending with corporate building maze

jokes.text: It's like a maze! I keep walking past the same... Sbarro's!
jokes.setup: Frank trying to leave the building
jokes.punchline: Multiple Sbarro's as evidence of corporate maze-like building
jokes.explanation: Good final Frank moment - Sbarro as symbol of corporate sameness and his confusion

jokes.text: I'm a celebrity! Please!
jokes.punchline: Playing celebrity card despite being a writer
jokes.explanation: Good ending to Frank's exit with desperation
